content: OSFI Guideline B-15 (Climate Risk Management) establishes expectations for federally regulated financial institutions regarding climate-related risk management. While primarily targeting banks and insurers, its requirements are cascading to SMB borrowers through lending criteria. Key requirements include: (1) Publication of a formal climate transition plan with measurable targets. (2) Identification and assessment of physical and transition climate risks. (3) TCFD-aligned climate disclosures including governance, strategy, risk management, and metrics/targets. (4) Scenario analysis covering at least two climate scenarios (e.g., 1.5°C and 3°C warming). For Canadian SMBs seeking bank financing, demonstrating alignment with B-15 expectations significantly improves access to green lending products and lower interest rates. Gaps in B-15 alignment are common among SMBs and are typically addressable within 6-12 months with structured planning.
